prompt: Use $workflow to turn the AIGC teaching livestream material in projects/素材 and the learning-step guide in projects/内容 into a concise pure teaching video named ai-course-basics.
expected: Routes to teaching-cut, scans default paths, builds source manifest, source-preprocess plan with speed=1.1 or explicit exception, transcript or equivalent evidence, guide-step map, teaching cut plan, slice plan, combination-slice plan when not exempted, subtitle correction plan, HyperFrames composition or documented fallback, optimized long video, coherent slice series under final/slices/, combination slices under final/slices/combinations/ when in scope, semantically corrected final SRT files matched to final audio, long-film QA, slice QA, combination-slice QA, subtitle QA, and projects/输出/ai-course-basics/final/manifest.json.

prompt: Use $workflow to make a concise pure teaching video from the AIGC livestream material in projects/素材. No teaching-step guide or course notes were provided.
expected: Routes to source-derived-guide, scans projects/素材, creates source preprocessing and round/source-unit evidence, creates transcript or equivalent evidence, derives source-backed learning steps from audio/video semantics, writes 02-guide-alignment/derived-learning-steps.md, maps selected long-film segments plus sequential and combination slices to those derived steps, prepares subtitle timing projection and semantic correction evidence, and continues through HyperFrames or a documented fallback to produce optimized long video, final/slices/, final/slices/combinations/ when in scope, and corrected final subtitles.

prompt: Use $workflow on a process tutorial source where the teacher shows a final result, lists five steps, demonstrates one or two examples of a repeated operation, gives model parameters, and shows the final output.
expected: Keeps a source-backed outcome hook, roadmap, step demos, rationale or parameter notes, repetition-collapse rationale, and final proof; selected segments include segment roles and the review can fail with FAIL-TEACHING-SHAPE if those source-supported parts are missing.

prompt: Use $workflow on a 70-minute screen-recorded teaching session. Produce the complete optimized long teaching film and a series of coherent teaching slices.
expected: Applies the Dual Output Contract, writes 03-edit-plan/teaching-cut-plan.md, 03-edit-plan/slice-plan.md with content-boundary start/end rationale, and 03-edit-plan/combination-slice-plan.md unless explicitly exempted, renders final/[任务名].mp4 and final/slices/[NN]-[slice-slug].mp4 files plus final/slices/combinations/ when in scope, writes slice indexes, QA-checks the long film and every sequential/combination slice, and fails with FAIL-DUAL-OUTPUT if any required deliverable set is missing, any slice lacks core explanation/context/source evidence, or slice boundaries were chosen by average duration/fixed chunks rather than teaching content.

prompt: Use $workflow on one long livestream source that repeats the same teaching loop three times. Apply the default 1.1x preprocessing, split repeated rounds, and maximize non-repetitive slice reuse.
expected: Applies the Source Material Preprocessing Contract and Combination Slice Utilization Contract, writes 01-understanding/source-preprocess-plan.md with speed=1.1 or an explicit exception, writes 01-understanding/round-map.json with source units such as -1/-2/-3, plans long-film outputs per usable source unit, writes 03-edit-plan/combination-slice-plan.md with semantic A, B1-B5, and C bands, creates 3-5 viable B candidates per interval or documents why not, records controlled random seed/selection or order-preserving recombination choices and non-repetition in the combination manifest, renders final/slices/combinations/ unless explicitly exempted or blocked with evidence, and fails with FAIL-SOURCE-PREPROCESS or FAIL-COMBINATION-SLICES if speed/timestamp mapping, round recognition, candidate pools, random/order-preserving selection, or combination QA is missing.

prompt: Use $workflow on a long teaching livestream with many coherent subtopics and examples. For every source-unit round, keep the content continuous and coherent, but maximize the number of output slices.
expected: Applies the Slice Quantity Maximization Contract, writes a slice opportunity inventory per source unit, records candidate_count, sequential_output_count, combination_candidate_count, combination_output_count, and excluded_count, outputs every coherent source-backed slice opportunity or places it into a combination pool unless there is a documented exclusion reason, avoids arbitrary quantity caps, preserves content-boundary starts and natural endings for each slice, and fails with FAIL-SLICE-QUANTITY if the run only outputs a representative subset, omits excluded-slice rationale, or drops viable coherent slices for convenience.

prompt: Repair an existing $workflow output: the final videos have matching SRT files but no subtitles appear on screen, and the slice series feels like average-duration chunks instead of complete teaching segments.
expected: Routes to repair-review, traces missing visible subtitles to N5/N6 and average-duration slice boundaries to N3, updates the source-layer rules if needed, rerenders the long film and every slice with visible captions unless an explicit sidecar-only exception exists, replans slices around content-driven starts and natural endings, records subtitle_render_mode and sampled-frame caption QA, and fails with FAIL-SUBTITLE-STYLE or FAIL-DUAL-OUTPUT if visible captions or content-boundary slice evidence is missing.

prompt: Use $workflow to render a teaching video that needs narration captions, chapter titles, step labels, parameter callouts, resource notes, emphasis keywords, and a final proof caption.
expected: Applies the Subtitle Style Contract, records 05-hyperframes/subtitle-style-spec.md, keeps source UI readable, renders narration captions at the default 100 px for 1080p output and scales proportionally by output height unless an exact user override is recorded, keeps captions bottom-aligned and single-line per cue, splits long caption text into short consecutive cues instead of renderer wrapping, samples frames for readability and overlap QA, and fails with FAIL-SUBTITLE-STYLE for wrong-size, low-contrast, non-bottom, wrapped, obstructive, mistimed, inconsistent, or uncategorized text overlays.

prompt: Repair an existing $workflow output: subtitles now appear, but the font is clearly too small, captions sit too high, and a single caption wraps into two lines. Keep captions at the bottom and split long subtitles instead of wrapping.
expected: Routes to repair-review, traces the issue to FAIL-SUBTITLE-STYLE at N5/N6, tightens source-layer subtitle style rules if needed, regenerates visible-render subtitle inputs as single-line cue fragments, rerenders the long film and every slice with the default narration-caption size computed from 100 px at 1080p and scaled by output height unless an exact user override is recorded, keeps captions bottom-aligned with no renderer-wrapped narration cue, records output resolution, font size, safe margin, line policy, and sampled-frame QA, and fails if any sampled caption uses the wrong size, is not bottom-aligned, or wraps within one cue.

prompt: Use $workflow to render a Chinese screen-recorded teaching video with visible narration subtitles, without specifying any custom subtitle font size.
expected: Applies the source-layer default narration-caption font size of exactly 100 px at 1080p, scales proportionally for other output heights, splits long subtitles into short single-line cue fragments, records the default font size in 05-hyperframes/subtitle-style-spec.md, final manifest, and sampled-frame QA, and fails with FAIL-SUBTITLE-STYLE if the rendered output silently uses a smaller default, wraps a cue, or omits readability/obstruction evidence.

prompt: Repair the current $workflow delivery and set the visible subtitle font size exactly to 20 while keeping captions bottom-aligned and single-line.
expected: Treats the user-specified font size as an exact override for the current delivery, records the override in subtitle-style spec, manifest, and QA, regenerates visible-render inputs if needed, rerenders the long film and every slice with font size 20, bottom alignment, and one visual line per cue, and fails with FAIL-SUBTITLE-STYLE if the rendered outputs use a different font size, wrap a cue, or omit sampled-frame evidence.

prompt: Repair the current $workflow delivery and change the visible subtitle color to beige while preserving the existing font size, bottom alignment, and single-line cue policy.
expected: Treats the user-specified caption color as an exact style override for the current delivery, records the color value in subtitle-style spec, manifest, and sampled-frame QA, regenerates visible-render inputs if needed, rerenders the long film and every slice with beige visible captions, and fails with FAIL-SUBTITLE-STYLE if the rendered outputs use the wrong caption color, omit visible captions, or lose the existing bottom-aligned single-line style.

prompt: Use $workflow on a Chinese screen-recorded teaching session. The final long film and every slice need subtitles that match the final edited audio and fix ASR homophones, tool names, and domain terms.
expected: Applies the Semantic Subtitle Processing Contract, Subtitle Display Proofing Contract, and Visible Subtitle Rendering Contract, keeps raw ASR as intermediate evidence only, writes 04-assets/subtitles/subtitle-correction-plan.md, display-proofing evidence, and a timing map or equivalent, produces final/[任务名].srt and matching final/slices/*.srt files, renders visible captions into final MP4s by default, QA-checks cue order/overlap/duration, cue display completeness, sampled-frame visibility, and high-risk terminology, and fails with FAIL-SUBTITLE-PROCESSING, FAIL-SUBTITLE-DISPLAY-PROOFING, or FAIL-SUBTITLE-STYLE if final subtitles are raw ASR, use the wrong timeline, lack display proofing, lack semantic correction evidence, or do not appear in the final videos.

prompt: Repair an existing $workflow output where the SRT timestamps exist, but visible subtitle cues cut sentences mid-phrase. First decide whether the short slices need rerendering.
expected: Routes to repair-review, applies the Subtitle Display Proofing Contract after semantic correction and final-timeline projection, writes 04-assets/subtitles/display-proofing-plan.md or equivalent with split/merge/time-shift decisions, creates display-proofed subtitle inputs, records rerender_scope as none, affected-only, all-subtitled-videos, or blocked, and fails with FAIL-SUBTITLE-DISPLAY-PROOFING if cues remain semantically incomplete, source-timeline cues are reused after recombination without final-timeline proofing, or the run blindly rerenders every short slice without affected-scope evidence.

prompt: Use $workflow on a teaching source where the screen operation is valuable but the original audio is noisy and the cleaned subtitle text should be used as a short voiceover.
expected: N3 writes a source-backed voiceover need/rationale and approved script, N4 loads ../cli/mmx-cli/SKILL.md to synthesize versioned audio under projects/素材/, records 04-assets/generated-audio-manifest.json and audio-text alignment, N5 uses the generated audio only on planned ranges, and review can fail with FAIL-GENERATED-AUDIO if the script, file path, manifest, source backing, sync, or mix QA is missing.

prompt: Use $workflow. There are several unrelated live videos and several learning guides under the default folders.
expected: Routes to ambiguous-batch, stops after N1/N7 with a clarification packet listing candidate task pairings and does not create a final teaching package.

prompt: Review the existing output in projects/输出/aigc-workshop because it is too long and still contains livestream chatter.
expected: Routes to repair-review, runs R1, checks QA against guide coverage and concision, returns to N3 for teaching-plan repair or N6 for QA evidence, and reports the fail code and rework target.
